content.text: Issue #44: metadata.json coverage stat misleading for large libraries. Let me read the three affected files.

content.content:
1‚Üí# SKILL.md Section Structure
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üí## agentskills.io Compliant Format
     4‚Üí
     5‚Üí### Frontmatter (Required ‚Äî agentskills.io Compliance)
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üí```yaml
     8‚Üí---
     9‚Üíname: {skill-name}
    10‚Üídescription: >
    11‚Üí  {Trigger-optimized description of what the skill does and when to use it.
    12‚Üí  Include specific keywords for agent discovery.
    13‚Üí  Mention what NOT to use it for if applicable.
    14‚Üí  1-1024 characters.}
    15‚Üí---
    16‚Üí```
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí**Frontmatter rules (agentskills.io specification):**
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí- `name`: 1-64 characters, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, must match parent directory name
    21‚Üí- `description`: 1-1024 characters, trigger-optimized for agent matching
    22‚Üí- Only 6 fields permitted: `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools`
    23‚Üí- `version` and `author` belong in metadata.json, NOT in frontmatter
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí### Two-Tier Section Structure
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíSKILL.md uses a two-tier structure to ensure actionable content survives `split-body` extraction.
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí#### Tier 1 ‚Äî Always Inline (survives split-body, target <300 lines)
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí| # | Section | Budget | Purpose |
    32‚Üí|---|---------|--------|---------|
    33‚Üí| 1 | **Overview** | ~10 lines | What the library does, source repo, version, tier, export count |
    34‚Üí| 2 | **Quick Start** | ~30 lines | 3-5 core functions with one runnable end-to-end example |
    35‚Üí| 3 | **Common Workflows** | ~30 lines | 4-5 typical function call sequences for common tasks |
    36‚Üí| 4 | **Key API Summary** | ~20 lines | Table of top 10-15 functions (name, purpose, key params) |
    37‚Üí| 4b | **Migration & Deprecation Warnings** | ~10 lines | T2-future warnings inline (Deep tier only, skip if none) |
    38‚Üí| 5 | **Key Types** | ~20 lines | Most important enum/type values inline |
    39‚Üí| 6 | **Architecture at a Glance** | ~10 lines | Bullet list of subsystem categories |
    40‚Üí| 7 | **CLI** | ~10 lines | Basic CLI commands (skip if no CLI) |
    41‚Üí| 8 | **Manual Sections** | ~5 lines | `<!-- [MANUAL] -->` markers for update-skill |
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí#### Tier 2 ‚Äî Reference-Eligible (extracted by split-body into references/)
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí| # | Section | Purpose |
    46‚Üí|---|---------|---------|
    47‚Üí| 9 | **Full API Reference** | Complete signatures, parameter tables, return types, examples, citations |
    48‚Üí| 10 | **Full Type Definitions** | All types, interfaces, enums with full field details |
    49‚Üí| 11 | **Full Integration Patterns** | Co-import patterns, adapter details, pipeline internals (Forge/Deep only) |
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí#### Assembly Rules
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí- Tier 1 sections are assembled first ‚Äî they form the standalone body
    54‚Üí- Tier 2 sections are assembled after ‚Äî they are progressive disclosure detail
    55‚Üí- Tier 1 sections are kept short enough that `split-body` targets the larger Tier 2 sections (`## Full` headings) into `references/`
    56‚Üí- After split-body, SKILL.md retains all Tier 1 content ‚Äî actionable without loading references
    57‚Üí- An agent loading only SKILL.md (no references) must get enough to act
    58‚Üí- **Section 4b (Migration & Deprecation Warnings)** is conditional: only emitted for Deep tier when T2-future annotations exist. Quick/Forge tiers and Deep tiers without T2-future annotations omit it entirely (no empty section). Parsers and validators must treat this section as optional.
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí### Provenance Citation Format
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí| Tier | Format | Example |
    63‚Üí|------|--------|---------|
    64‚Üí| T1 (AST) | `[AST:{file}:L{line}]` | `[AST:src/auth/index.ts:L42]` |
    65‚Üí| T1-low (Source) | `[SRC:{file}:L{line}]` | `[SRC:src/auth/index.ts:L42]` |
    66‚Üí| T2 (QMD) | `[QMD:{collection}:{doc}]` | `[QMD:project:CHANGELOG.md]` |
    67‚Üí| T3 (External) | `[EXT:{url}]` | `[EXT:docs.example.com/api]` |
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí### [MANUAL] Section Markers
    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíSeed empty manual sections for future update-skill compatibility:
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí```markdown
    74‚Üí<!-- [MANUAL:additional-notes] -->
    75‚Üí<!-- Add custom notes here. This section is preserved during skill updates. -->
    76‚Üí<!-- [/MANUAL:additional-notes] -->
    77‚Üí```
    78‚Üí
    79‚ÜíPlace after Quick Start and after API Reference sections.
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí---
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí## context-snippet.md Format (Vercel-aligned indexed format)
    84‚Üí
    85‚ÜíIndexed pipe-delimited format for CLAUDE.md managed section (~80-120 tokens per skill). Aligned with Vercel's research finding that retrieval instructions + indexed file maps + inline gotchas dramatically improve agent performance.
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üí```markdown
    88‚Üí[{skill-name} v{version}]|root: skills/{skill-name}/
    89‚Üí|IMPORTANT: {skill-name} v{version} ‚Äî read SKILL.md before writing {skill-name} code. Do NOT rely on training data.
    90‚Üí|quick-start:{SKILL.md#quick-start}
    91‚Üí|api: {top exports with () for functions, comma-separated}
    92‚Üí|key-types:{SKILL.md#key-types} ‚Äî {inline summary of most important type values}
    93‚Üí|gotchas: {2-3 most critical pitfalls or breaking changes, inline}
    94‚Üí```
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí### Format rules
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí- **Line 1:** Skill name + version + root path ‚Äî version signals training data staleness
    99‚Üí- **Line 2 (IMPORTANT):** Retrieval instruction ‚Äî always present, tells agent to read SKILL.md before acting
   100‚Üí- **Lines 3+:** Pipe-delimited index mapping topics to SKILL.md sections (with `#anchor` pointers)
   101‚Üí- **Inline content:** Each index line includes a brief inline summary (~10 words) so the agent can decide whether to read the full section
   102‚Üí- **gotchas line:** 2-3 most critical pitfalls inline ‚Äî prevents mistakes without requiring a file read
   103‚Üí- **Token budget:** ~80-120 tokens per skill (justified by Vercel's finding that indexed format maintains performance at 80% compression)
   104‚Üí- **T1-now content only** ‚Äî no T2 annotations in the snippet
   105‚Üí- **Section anchors** (`#quick-start`, `#key-types`) must match actual SKILL.md heading slugs
   106‚Üí- **Version** comes from source detection (per issue #32), not brief default
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üí---
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí## metadata.json Structure
   111‚Üí
   112‚Üí```json

   143‚Üí---

   145‚Üí## references/ Directory Structure

# SKILL.md Section Structure

## agentskills.io Compliant Format

### Frontmatter (Required ‚Äî agentskills.io Compliance)

```yaml
---
name: {skill-name}
description: >
  {Trigger-optimized description of what the skill does and when to use it.
  Include specific keywords for agent discovery.
  Mention what NOT to use it for if applicable.
  1-1024 characters.}
---
```

**Frontmatter rules (agentskills.io specification):**

- `name`: 1-64 characters, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, must match parent directory name
- `description`: 1-1024 characters, trigger-optimized for agent matching
- Only 6 fields permitted: `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools`
- `version` and `author` belong in metadata.json, NOT in frontmatter

### Two-Tier Section Structure

SKILL.md uses a two-tier structure to ensure actionable content survives `split-body` extraction.

#### Tier 1 ‚Äî Always Inline (survives split-body, target <300 lines)

| # | Section | Budget | Purpose |
|---|---------|--------|---------|
| 1 | **Overview** | ~10 lines | What the library does, source repo, version, tier, export count |
| 2 | **Quick Start** | ~30 lines | 3-5 core functions with one runnable end-to-end example |
| 3 | **Common Workflows** | ~30 lines | 4-5 typical function call sequences for common tasks |
| 4 | **Key API Summary** | ~20 lines | Table of top 10-15 functions (name, purpose, key params) |
| 4b | **Migration & Deprecation Warnings** | ~10 lines | T2-future warnings inline (Deep tier only, skip if none) |
| 5 | **Key Types** | ~20 lines | Most important enum/type values inline |
| 6 | **Architecture at a Glance** | ~10 lines | Bullet list of subsystem categories |
| 7 | **CLI** | ~10 lines | Basic CLI commands (skip if no CLI) |
| 8 | **Manual Sections** | ~5 lines | `<!-- [MANUAL] -->` markers for update-skill |

#### Tier 2 ‚Äî Reference-Eligible (extracted by split-body into references/)

| # | Section | Purpose |
|---|---------|---------|
| 9 | **Full API Reference** | Complete signatures, parameter tables, return types, examples, citations |
| 10 | **Full Type Definitions** | All types, interfaces, enums with full field details |
| 11 | **Full Integration Patterns** | Co-import patterns, adapter details, pipeline internals (Forge/Deep only) |

#### Assembly Rules

- Tier 1 sections are assembled first ‚Äî they form the standalone body
- Tier 2 sections are assembled after ‚Äî they are progressive disclosure detail
- Tier 1 sections are kept short enough that `split-body` targets the larger Tier 2 sections (`## Full` headings) into `references/`
- After split-body, SKILL.md retains all Tier 1 content ‚Äî actionable without loading references
- An agent loading only SKILL.md (no references) must get enough to act
- **Section 4b (Migration & Deprecation Warnings)** is conditional: only emitted for Deep tier when T2-future annotations exist. Quick/Forge tiers and Deep tiers without T2-future annotations omit it entirely (no empty section). Parsers and validators must treat this section as optional.

### Provenance Citation Format

| Tier | Format | Example |
|------|--------|---------|
| T1 (AST) | `[AST:{file}:L{line}]` | `[AST:src/auth/index.ts:L42]` |
| T1-low (Source) | `[SRC:{file}:L{line}]` | `[SRC:src/auth/index.ts:L42]` |
| T2 (QMD) | `[QMD:{collection}:{doc}]` | `[QMD:project:CHANGELOG.md]` |
| T3 (External) | `[EXT:{url}]` | `[EXT:docs.example.com/api]` |

### [MANUAL] Section Markers

Seed empty manual sections for future update-skill compatibility:

```markdown
<!-- [MANUAL:additional-notes] -->
<!-- Add custom notes here. This section is preserved during skill updates. -->
<!-- [/MANUAL:additional-notes] -->
```

Place after Quick Start and after API Reference sections.

---

## context-snippet.md Format (Vercel-aligned indexed format)

Indexed pipe-delimited format for CLAUDE.md managed section (~80-120 tokens per skill). Aligned with Vercel's research finding that retrieval instructions + indexed file maps + inline gotchas dramatically improve agent performance.

```markdown
[{skill-name} v{version}]|root: skills/{skill-name}/
|IMPORTANT: {skill-name} v{version} ‚Äî read SKILL.md before writing {skill-name} code. Do NOT rely on training data.
|quick-start:{SKILL.md#quick-start}
|api: {top exports with () for functions, comma-separated}
|key-types:{SKILL.md#key-types} ‚Äî {inline summary of most important type values}
|gotchas: {2-3 most critical pitfalls or breaking changes, inline}
```

### Format rules

- **Line 1:** Skill name + version + root path ‚Äî version signals training data staleness
- **Line 2 (IMPORTANT):** Retrieval instruction ‚Äî always present, tells agent to read SKILL.md before acting
- **Lines 3+:** Pipe-delimited index mapping topics to SKILL.md sections (with `#anchor` pointers)
- **Inline content:** Each index line includes a brief inline summary (~10 words) so the agent can decide whether to read the full section
- **gotchas line:** 2-3 most critical pitfalls inline ‚Äî prevents mistakes without requiring a file read
- **Token budget:** ~80-120 tokens per skill (justified by Vercel's finding that indexed format maintains performance at 80% compression)
- **T1-now content only** ‚Äî no T2 annotations in the snippet
- **Section anchors** (`#quick-start`, `#key-types`) must match actual SKILL.md heading slugs
- **Version** comes from source detection (per issue #32), not brief default

---

## metadata.json Structure

```json
{
  "name": "{skill-name}",
  "version": "{source-version}",
  "skill_type": "single",
  "source_authority": "{official|community|internal}",
  "source_repo": "{github-url}",
  "source_root": "{resolved-source-path}",
  "source_commit": "{commit-hash}",
  "confidence_tier": "{Quick|Forge|Deep}",
  "spec_version": "1.3",
  "generation_date": "{ISO-8601}",
  "exports": [],
  "tool_versions": {
    "ast_grep": "{version-or-null}",
    "qmd": "{version-or-null}",
    "skf": "1.0.0"
  },
  "stats": {
    "exports_documented": 0,
    "exports_total": 0,
    "coverage": 0.0,
    "confidence_t1": 0,
    "confidence_t2": 0,
    "confidence_t3": 0
  },
  "dependencies": [],
  "compatibility": "{semver-range}"
}
```

---

## references/ Directory Structure

One file per major function group or type:

```
references/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ {function-group-a}.md    ‚Äî Detailed reference with full examples
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ {function-group-b}.md    ‚Äî Detailed reference with full examples
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ {type-name}.md           ‚Äî Type definition details
```

Each reference file includes:
- Full function signatures
- Detailed parameter descriptions
- Return value details
- Complete usage examples
- Related functions cross-references
- Temporal annotations (Deep tier: T2-past, T2-future)

---

## provenance-map.json Structure

```json
{
  "skill_name": "{name}",
  "source_repo": "{url}",
  "source_commit": "{hash}",
  "generated_at": "{ISO-8601}",
  "entries": [
    {
      "claim": "getToken accepts userId and optional TokenOptions",
      "source_file": "src/auth/index.ts",
      "source_line": 42,
      "confidence": "T1",
      "extraction_method": "ast_bridge.scan_definitions",
      "ast_node_type": "export_function_declaration"
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## evidence-report.md Structure

```markdown
# Evidence Report: {skill-name}

**Generated:** {date}
**Forge Tier:** {tier}
**Source:** {repo} @ {commit}

## Tool Versions
- ast-grep: {version}
- QMD: {version}
- SKF: 1.0.0

## Extraction Summary
- Files scanned: {count}
- Exports found: {count}
- Confidence: T1={n}, T2={n}, T3={n}

## Validation Results
- Schema: {pass/fail}
- Frontmatter: {pass/fail}

## Warnings
- {any warnings from extraction or validation}
```

1‚Üí---
     2‚Üíname: 'step-05-compile'

     8‚Üí
     9‚Üí# Step 5: Compile
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí## STEP GOAL:
    12‚Üí
    13‚ÜíTo assemble the complete skill content from the extraction inventory and enrichment annotations ‚Äî building SKILL.md sections, context-snippet.md, metadata.json, and references/ content according to the agentskills.io format.
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üí## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üí### Universal Rules:
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí- üìñ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20‚Üí- üéØ ALWAYS follow the exact instructions in the step file
    21‚Üí- ‚öôÔ∏è TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    22‚Üí- ‚úÖ YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí### Role Reinforcement:
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí- ‚úÖ You are a skill compilation engine performing structured assembly
    27‚Üí- ‚úÖ Every instruction in SKILL.md must trace to source code with a provenance citation
    28‚Üí- ‚úÖ Uncitable content is excluded, not guessed ‚Äî zero hallucination
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí### Step-Specific Rules:
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí- üéØ Focus ONLY on assembling content from extraction inventory + enrichment
    33‚Üí- üö´ FORBIDDEN to include any content without a provenance citation
    34‚Üí- üíæ Sub-agents and compilation processes may write to a staging directory (e.g., `_bmad-output/{name}/`)
    35‚Üí- üö´ FORBIDDEN to write final files to `skills/` or `forge-data/` ‚Äî that's step-07
    36‚Üí- üö´ FORBIDDEN to fabricate examples not found in source tests or docs
    37‚Üí- ‚öíÔ∏è Seed `<!-- [MANUAL] -->` markers for future update-skill compatibility
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí- üéØ Follow MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    42‚Üí- üíæ Build all content in context or write to a staging directory (`_bmad-output/{name}/`)
    43‚Üí- üìñ Follow agentskills.io section structure from data file
    44‚Üí- üö´ Do not promote any output files to final `skills/` or `forge-data/` directories ‚Äî that's step-07
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí- Available: extraction_inventory, enrichment_annotations (if Deep), doc_fetch_inventory (if doc_urls), brief_data, tier
    49‚Üí- Focus: Assembling structured content from verified data
    50‚Üí- Docs-only mode: If `source_type: "docs-only"`, all content is T3 `[EXT:{url}]`. Overview must note "Generated from external documentation." metadata.json `source_authority` forced to `community`. No AST/source citations exist ‚Äî only `[EXT:...]` citations.
    51‚Üí- Limits: Do NOT write to final `skills/` or `forge-data/` directories, validate spec compliance, or report
    52‚Üí- Dependencies: Extraction inventory from step-03 (enrichment from step-04 if Deep)
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí### 1. Load Data Files
    59‚Üí
    60‚ÜíLoad `{skillSectionsData}` and `{assemblyRulesData}` completely. These define the agentskills.io-compliant format and detailed assembly rules for all output artifacts.
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí### 2. Build SKILL.md Content
    63‚Üí
    64‚ÜíAssemble each section in order using the assembly rules data file (`{assemblyRulesData}`). The data file specifies frontmatter format, Tier 1 section details (Sections 1-8), Tier 2 section details (Sections 9-11), and assembly ordering rules. Follow it exactly.
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí### 3. Build context-snippet.md Content
    67‚Üí
    68‚ÜíVercel-aligned indexed format for CLAUDE.md managed section (~80-120 tokens):
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí```markdown
    71‚Üí[{skill-name} v{version}]|root: skills/{skill-name}/
    72‚Üí|IMPORTANT: {skill-name} v{version} ‚Äî read SKILL.md before writing {skill-name} code. Do NOT rely on training data.
    73‚Üí|quick-start:{SKILL.md#quick-start}
    74‚Üí|api: {top exports with () for functions, comma-separated}
    75‚Üí|key-types:{SKILL.md#key-types} ‚Äî {inline summary of most important type values}
    76‚Üí|gotchas: {2-3 most critical pitfalls or breaking changes, inline}
    77‚Üí```
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí**Derivation rules:**
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí- **version**: From source detection (reconciled in step-03), not brief default
    82‚Üí- **api**: Top 10 exports from extraction inventory, append `()` to function names
    83‚Üí- **key-types**: Inline summary of most important enum/type values from Key Types section
    84‚Üí- **gotchas**: Derived from T2-future annotations (breaking changes), async requirements, version-specific behavior changes. If no gotchas available, omit the gotchas line.
    85‚Üí- **Section anchors** (`#quick-start`, `#key-types`): Must match actual heading slugs in the assembled SKILL.md
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üí### 4. Build metadata.json Content
    88‚Üí
    89‚ÜíFollowing the structure from the skill-sections data file:
    90‚Üí- Populate all fields from brief_data, extraction inventory, and tier
    91‚Üí- Set `generation_date` to current ISO-8601 timestamp
    92‚Üí- Set `source_commit` from resolved source (if available)
    93‚Üí- Set `stats` from extraction aggregate counts
    94‚Üí- Set `tool_versions` based on tier and available tools
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí### 5. Build references/ Content
    97‚Üí
    98‚ÜíCreate one reference file per major function group or type:
    99‚Üí- Full function signatures with detailed parameter descriptions
   100‚Üí- Complete usage examples (from source only)
   101‚Üí- Related functions cross-references
   102‚Üí- Temporal annotations (Deep tier: T2-past, T2-future)
   103‚Üí
   104‚ÜíGroup functions logically by module, file, or functional area.
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí### 6. Build provenance-map.json Content
   107‚Üí
   108‚ÜíOne entry per extracted claim: claim text, source_file, source_line, confidence tier (T1/T1-low/T2), extraction_method, ast_node_type. See `{skillSectionsData}` for full schema.
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí### 7. Build evidence-report.md Content
   111‚Üí
   112‚ÜíCompilation audit trail: generation date, forge tier, source info, tool versions, extraction summary (files/exports/confidence), validation results (populated in step-06), warnings. See `{skillSectionsData}` for full template.
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí### 8. Menu Handling Logic
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí**Auto-proceed step ‚Äî no user interaction.**
   117‚Üí
   118‚ÜíAfter all content is assembled in context (or written to the staging directory), immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí#### EXECUTION RULES:
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üí- This is an auto-proceed assembly step with no user choices
   123‚Üí- All content stays in context or in the staging directory ‚Äî no final files are written yet

   127‚Üí

Note: extraction-rules.yaml is generated by step-07 from extraction data, not assembled here.
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üí---
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üí## üö® SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   133‚Üí
   134‚Üí### ‚úÖ SUCCESS:
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üí- Skill-sections data file and assembly rules data file loaded and followed
   137‚Üí- SKILL.md assembled with all required sections in agentskills.io format
   138‚Üí- Tier 1 sections assembled first and kept under 300 lines (excluding frontmatter)
   139‚Üí- Tier 2 sections assembled after all Tier 1 sections
   140‚Üí- Section 4b emitted only when Deep tier AND T2-future annotations exist; omitted entirely otherwise (no empty section)
   141‚Üí- Every function entry has a provenance citation
   142‚Üí- [MANUAL] markers seeded for update-skill compatibility
   143‚Üí- context-snippet.md, metadata.json, references/, provenance-map.json, evidence-report.md all assembled
   144‚Üí- No content without provenance citations included
   145‚Üí- Auto-proceeded to step-06
   146‚Üí
   147‚Üí### ‚ùå SYSTEM FAILURE:
   148‚Üí
   149‚Üí- Including functions or examples without provenance citations
   150‚Üí- Fabricating usage examples not found in source
   151‚Üí- Writing files to final `skills/` or `forge-data/` directories (that's step-07)
   152‚Üí- Missing required SKILL.md sections
   153‚Üí- Emitting an empty Section 4b when no T2-future annotations exist
   154‚Üí- Tier 1 content exceeding 300 lines without reduction
   155‚Üí- Assembling Tier 2 sections before Tier 1 sections

file.content:
---
name: 'step-05-compile'
description: 'Assemble SKILL.md, context-snippet, metadata, and references from extraction inventory'
nextStepFile: './step-06-validate.md'
skillSectionsData: '../data/skill-sections.md'
assemblyRulesData: '../data/compile-assembly-rules.md'
---

# Step 5: Compile

## STEP GOAL:

To assemble the complete skill content from the extraction inventory and enrichment annotations ‚Äî building SKILL.md sections, context-snippet.md, metadata.json, and references/ content according to the agentskills.io format.

## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):

### Universal Rules:

- üìñ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
- üéØ ALWAYS follow the exact instructions in the step file
- ‚öôÔ∏è TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
- ‚úÖ YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`

### Role Reinforcement:

- ‚úÖ You are a skill compilation engine performing structured assembly
- ‚úÖ Every instruction in SKILL.md must trace to source code with a provenance citation
- ‚úÖ Uncitable content is excluded, not guessed ‚Äî zero hallucination

### Step-Specific Rules:

- üéØ Focus ONLY on assembling content from extraction inventory + enrichment
- üö´ FORBIDDEN to include any content without a provenance citation
- üíæ Sub-agents and compilation processes may write to a staging directory (e.g., `_bmad-output/{name}/`)
- üö´ FORBIDDEN to write final files to `skills/` or `forge-data/` ‚Äî that's step-07
- üö´ FORBIDDEN to fabricate examples not found in source tests or docs
- ‚öíÔ∏è Seed `<!-- [MANUAL] -->` markers for future update-skill compatibility

## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:

- üéØ Follow MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
- üíæ Build all content in context or write to a staging directory (`_bmad-output/{name}/`)
- üìñ Follow agentskills.io section structure from data file
- üö´ Do not promote any output files to final `skills/` or `forge-data/` directories ‚Äî that's step-07

## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:

- Available: extraction_inventory, enrichment_annotations (if Deep), doc_fetch_inventory (if doc_urls), brief_data, tier
- Focus: Assembling structured content from verified data
- Docs-only mode: If `source_type: "docs-only"`, all content is T3 `[EXT:{url}]`. Overview must note "Generated from external documentation." metadata.json `source_authority` forced to `community`. No AST/source citations exist ‚Äî only `[EXT:...]` citations.
- Limits: Do NOT write to final `skills/` or `forge-data/` directories, validate spec compliance, or report
- Dependencies: Extraction inventory from step-03 (enrichment from step-04 if Deep)

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.

### 1. Load Data Files

Load `{skillSectionsData}` and `{assemblyRulesData}` completely. These define the agentskills.io-compliant format and detailed assembly rules for all output artifacts.

### 2. Build SKILL.md Content

Assemble each section in order using the assembly rules data file (`{assemblyRulesData}`). The data file specifies frontmatter format, Tier 1 section details (Sections 1-8), Tier 2 section details (Sections 9-11), and assembly ordering rules. Follow it exactly.

### 3. Build context-snippet.md Content

Vercel-aligned indexed format for CLAUDE.md managed section (~80-120 tokens):

```markdown
[{skill-name} v{version}]|root: skills/{skill-name}/
|IMPORTANT: {skill-name} v{version} ‚Äî read SKILL.md before writing {skill-name} code. Do NOT rely on training data.
|quick-start:{SKILL.md#quick-start}
|api: {top exports with () for functions, comma-separated}
|key-types:{SKILL.md#key-types} ‚Äî {inline summary of most important type values}
|gotchas: {2-3 most critical pitfalls or breaking changes, inline}
```

**Derivation rules:**

- **version**: From source detection (reconciled in step-03), not brief default
- **api**: Top 10 exports from extraction inventory, append `()` to function names
- **key-types**: Inline summary of most important enum/type values from Key Types section
- **gotchas**: Derived from T2-future annotations (breaking changes), async requirements, version-specific behavior changes. If no gotchas available, omit the gotchas line.
- **Section anchors** (`#quick-start`, `#key-types`): Must match actual heading slugs in the assembled SKILL.md

### 4. Build metadata.json Content

Following the structure from the skill-sections data file:
- Populate all fields from brief_data, extraction inventory, and tier
- Set `generation_date` to current ISO-8601 timestamp
- Set `source_commit` from resolved source (if available)
- Set `stats` from extraction aggregate counts
- Set `tool_versions` based on tier and available tools

### 5. Build references/ Content

Create one reference file per major function group or type:
- Full function signatures with detailed parameter descriptions
- Complete usage examples (from source only)
- Related functions cross-references
- Temporal annotations (Deep tier: T2-past, T2-future)

Group functions logically by module, file, or functional area.

### 6. Build provenance-map.json Content

One entry per extracted claim: claim text, source_file, source_line, confidence tier (T1/T1-low/T2), extraction_method, ast_node_type. See `{skillSectionsData}` for full schema.

### 7. Build evidence-report.md Content

Compilation audit trail: generation date, forge tier, source info, tool versions, extraction summary (files/exports/confidence), validation results (populated in step-06), warnings. See `{skillSectionsData}` for full template.

### 8. Menu Handling Logic

**Auto-proceed step ‚Äî no user interaction.**

After all content is assembled in context (or written to the staging directory), immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`.

#### EXECUTION RULES:

- This is an auto-proceed assembly step with no user choices
- All content stays in context or in the staging directory ‚Äî no final files are written yet
- Proceed directly to validation after assembly is complete

## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE

ONLY WHEN all 6 content artifacts (SKILL.md, context-snippet.md, metadata.json, references/, provenance-map.json, evidence-report.md) are assembled in context will you proceed to load `{nextStepFile}` for spec validation. Note: extraction-rules.yaml is generated by step-07 from extraction data, not assembled here.

---

## üö® SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS

### ‚úÖ SUCCESS:

- Skill-sections data file and assembly rules data file loaded and followed
- SKILL.md assembled with all required sections in agentskills.io format
- Tier 1 sections assembled first and kept under 300 lines (excluding frontmatter)
- Tier 2 sections assembled after all Tier 1 sections
- Section 4b emitted only when Deep tier AND T2-future annotations exist; omitted entirely otherwise (no empty section)
- Every function entry has a provenance citation
- [MANUAL] markers seeded for update-skill compatibility
- context-snippet.md, metadata.json, references/, provenance-map.json, evidence-report.md all assembled
- No content without provenance citations included
- Auto-proceeded to step-06

### ‚ùå SYSTEM FAILURE:

- Including functions or examples without provenance citations
- Fabricating usage examples not found in source
- Writing files to final `skills/` or `forge-data/` directories (that's step-07)
- Missing required SKILL.md sections
- Emitting an empty Section 4b when no T2-future annotations exist
- Tier 1 content exceeding 300 lines without reduction
- Assembling Tier 2 sections before Tier 1 sections
- Not seeding [MANUAL] markers
- Not building all 6 content artifacts (extraction-rules.yaml is built by step-07)

**Master Rule:** Zero hallucination ‚Äî every line of SKILL.md must trace to source code. Compile from data, not imagination.

content.content:
1‚Üí---
     2‚Üíname: 'step-06-validate'
     3‚Üídescription: 'Validate compiled skill content against agentskills.io spec via skill-check'
     4‚ÜínextStepFile: './step-07-generate-artifacts.md'
     5‚Üí---
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üí# Step 6: Validate
     8‚Üí
     9‚Üí## STEP GOAL:
    10‚Üí
    11‚ÜíTo validate the compiled SKILL.md content against the agentskills.io specification using skill-check, auto-fix any validation failures, and confirm spec compliance before artifact generation.
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üí## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üí### Universal Rules:
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üí- üìñ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    18‚Üí- üéØ ALWAYS follow the exact instructions in the step file
    19‚Üí- ‚öôÔ∏è TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    20‚Üí- ‚úÖ YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí### Role Reinforcement:
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí- ‚úÖ You are a skill compilation engine performing quality assurance
    25‚Üí- ‚úÖ Validation ensures spec compliance ‚Äî it does not modify content semantics
    26‚Üí- ‚úÖ Tool unavailability means skip validation, not halt workflow
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí### Step-Specific Rules:
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí- üéØ Focus ONLY on validating compiled content against spec
    31‚Üí- üö´ FORBIDDEN to add new content ‚Äî only fix spec compliance issues
    32‚Üí- üíæ Validation and auto-fix modify files in the staging directory (`_bmad-output/{name}/`)
    33‚Üí- üí¨ If auto-fix fails, report issues clearly but proceed (warn, don't halt)
    34‚Üí- ‚öôÔ∏è If skill-check unavailable: skip validation, add warning to evidence report
    35‚Üí- ‚öÝÔ∏è Ignore non-zero exit codes from `skill-check` if the JSON output shows 0 errors ‚Äî parse JSON output, not exit codes
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí- üéØ Follow MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    40‚Üí- üíæ Validation results are added to evidence-report content in context
    41‚Üí- üìñ Auto-fix pattern: validate ‚Üí fix ‚Üí re-validate (once)
    42‚Üí- üö´ Maximum one auto-fix attempt per validation failure
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí- Available: All compiled content from step-05 (SKILL.md, metadata.json, etc.)
    47‚Üí- Focus: Spec compliance validation and auto-fix
    48‚Üí- Limits: Do NOT add new content or modify extraction data
    49‚Üí- Dependencies: Compiled content must exist from step-05
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí### 1. Check Tool Availability
    56‚Üí
    57‚ÜíRun: `npx skill-check -h`
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí- If succeeds: Continue to automated validation (section 2)
    60‚Üí- If fails: Perform manual fallback (section 3); add note to evidence-report: "Spec validation performed manually ‚Äî skill-check tool unavailable"
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí**Important:** Do not assume availability ‚Äî empirical check required.
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí### 2. Validate & Auto-Fix (skill-check check --fix)
    65‚Üí
    66‚ÜíRun the external skill-check tool against the compiled skill staging directory:
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí```bash
    69‚Üínpx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --fix --format json --no-security-scan
    70‚Üí```
    71‚Üí
    72‚ÜíThis performs frontmatter validation, description quality checks, body limit enforcement, local link resolution, file formatting, auto-fix of deterministic issues, and quality scoring (0-100) across five weighted categories.
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí**Parse the JSON output** for: `qualityScore` (0-100), `diagnostics[]` (remaining issues), `fixed[]` (auto-corrected issues).
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí**Note:** `skill-check` may return non-zero exit code even when `errorCount` is 0. Always rely on parsed JSON, not the shell exit code.
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí- **Score ‚â• 70:** Record "Schema: PASS (score: {score}/100)" in evidence-report
    79‚Üí- **Score < 70:** Log remaining diagnostics as warnings, record "Schema: WARN ‚Äî score {score}/100, {count} remaining issues", proceed
    80‚Üí- **Unfixable errors:** Record specific rule IDs and suggestions, proceed with warnings
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí### 3. Validate Frontmatter (Fallback)
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí**If skill-check was available:** Skip ‚Äî already validated in step 2.
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí**If skill-check NOT available (fallback):** Perform manual frontmatter compliance check:
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí- [ ] Frontmatter present ‚Äî file starts with `---` and has closing `---`
    89‚Üí- [ ] `name` field ‚Äî present, non-empty, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, 1-64 chars
    90‚Üí- [ ] `name` matches skill output directory name
    91‚Üí- [ ] `description` field ‚Äî present, non-empty, 1-1024 characters
    92‚Üí- [ ] No unknown fields ‚Äî only `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools` permitted
    93‚Üí- [ ] `version` and `author` are NOT in frontmatter (they belong in metadata.json)
    94‚Üí
    95‚ÜíIf fails: auto-fix (deterministic), re-validate once, record result. If passes: record "Frontmatter: PASS".
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí### 4. Split Oversized Body (if needed)
    98‚Üí
    99‚Üí**If step 2 reported `body.max_lines` failure:**
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí```bash
   102‚Üínpx skill-check split-body <staging-skill-dir> --write
   103‚Üí```
   104‚Üí
   105‚ÜíThen re-validate: `npx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --format json --no-security-scan`
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí**If skill-check unavailable or no body size issue:** Skip.
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí### 5. Security Scan
   110‚Üí
   111‚Üí**If skill-check available:**
   112‚Üí
   113‚Üí```bash
   114‚Üínpx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --format json
   115‚Üí```
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí(Security scan enabled by default when `--no-security-scan` omitted. The scan uses [Snyk Agent Scan](https://github.com/snyk/agent-scan) to check for prompt injection risks, sensitive data exposure, and unsafe tool permissions.)
   118‚Üí
   119‚ÜíRecord any security warnings in evidence-report. Security findings are advisory ‚Äî they do not block artifact generation.
   120‚Üí
   121‚Üí**If security scan fails due to missing SNYK_TOKEN:**
   122‚Üí
   123‚ÜíDisplay actionable guidance:
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí"Security scan requires a Snyk API token. **Important:** The Snyk API requires an Enterprise plan ([authentication docs](https://docs.snyk.io/snyk-api/authentication-for-api)). To enable:
   126‚Üí1. Obtain a Snyk Enterprise account with API access
   127‚Üí2. Get your API token from Account Settings ‚Üí API Token
   128‚Üí3. Set `SNYK_TOKEN=your-token` in your environment or `.env`
   129‚Üí4. Re-run [SF] Setup Forge to update tool detection, then re-run [CS] Create Skill
   130‚Üí
   131‚ÜíIf you don't have an Enterprise account, use `--no-security-scan` flag in skill-check to skip, or `--security-scan-runner pipx|uvx|local` to control the execution method. Security scanning is optional and does not affect tier level or block skill compilation."
   132‚Üí
   133‚ÜíRecord: "Security scan skipped ‚Äî SNYK_TOKEN not configured"
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üí**If skill-check unavailable:** Skip with note: "Security scan skipped ‚Äî skill-check tool unavailable"
   136‚Üí
   137‚Üí### 6. Content Quality Review (tessl)
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üí**If tessl available**, run: `npx -y tessl skill review <staging-skill-dir>`
   140‚Üí
   141‚ÜíParse output for: `description_score`, `content_score`, `average_score`, `validation_result`, `judge_suggestions[]`.
   142‚Üí
   143‚Üí- **Content score < 70%:** Record warning: "Content quality warning: tessl scored content at {score}%."
   144‚Üí- **Unavailable:** Skip with note: "Content quality review skipped ‚Äî tessl tool unavailable"
   145‚Üí
   146‚Üí**Expected scoring for two-tier design:** The Skill Forge two-tier design (Tier 1 Key API Summary + Tier 2 Full API Reference) intentionally includes progressive disclosure. tessl's `conciseness` scorer may flag this as redundancy (typically scoring 2/3), which is expected behavior ‚Äî not a defect. Content scores >= 60% are acceptable when both tiers are correctly applied with proper differentiation (see step-05 Tier 2 assembly rules). Do NOT consolidate Tier 1 and Tier 2 content to improve the score ‚Äî the two-tier structure is a deliberate design choice for standalone usability.
   147‚Üí
   148‚Üítessl installs automatically via `npx`. A missing tool is not an error ‚Äî graceful skip.
   149‚Üí
   150‚Üí#### 6b. User Decision Gate (conditional)
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üí**If tessl returned no suggestions OR tessl was unavailable:** Skip this gate ‚Äî auto-proceed.
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí**If tessl returned suggestions**, present them to the user:
   155‚Üí
   156‚Üí"**Content quality review: {score}%**
   157‚Üí
   158‚Üítessl suggestions:
   159‚Üí{numbered list of judge_suggestions}
   160‚Üí
   161‚Üí**Select an option:**
   162‚Üí- **[S] Skip** ‚Äî proceed with current content as-is (default)
   163‚Üí- **[A] Apply structural fixes** ‚Äî apply only structural suggestions (split sections, consolidate duplicates). No new content generated.
   164‚Üí- **[R] Review all** ‚Äî show each suggestion with proposed changes before applying"
   165‚Üí
   166‚Üí#### Gate Rules:
   167‚Üí
   168‚Üí- **Structural suggestions** (split reference section, consolidate duplicates, reorder sections) can be applied without zero-hallucination risk ‚Äî they restructure existing content
   169‚Üí- **Semantic suggestions** (add examples, add error handling, add validation checkpoints) introduce content not verified from source code. If the user chooses to apply these:
   170‚Üí  - Warn: "This adds content not verified from source code."
   171‚Üí  - Mark applied content with `<!-- [TESSL:auto-fix] -->` markers
   172‚Üí  - Cite as `[TESSL:suggestion]` in the provenance map with `confidence: "TESSL"` (below T3)
   173‚Üí  - Record in evidence report: "TESSL-suggested content applied: {count} items (unverified)"
   174‚Üí- **If user selects [S]:** Record "tessl suggestions: skipped by user" in evidence report. Proceed to section 7.
   175‚Üí- **If user selects [A]:** Apply structural fixes only, re-run tessl to capture updated score, record results. Proceed to section 7.
   176‚Üí- **If user selects [R]:** Show each suggestion with the proposed change. For each, user confirms or skips. Apply confirmed changes, record results. Proceed to section 7.
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí### 7. Validate metadata.json
   179‚Üí
   180‚ÜíCross-check metadata.json against extraction inventory:
   181‚Üí- `stats.exports_documented` / `stats.exports_total` / `stats.coverage` are accurate
   182‚Üí- `confidence_t1`, `confidence_t2`, `confidence_t3` match actual counts
   183‚Üí- `spec_version` is "1.3"
   184‚Üí
   185‚ÜíAuto-fix any discrepancies (these are computed values).
   186‚Üí
   187‚Üí### 8. Update Evidence Report
   188‚Üí
   189‚ÜíAdd validation results to evidence-report content in context:
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üí```markdown
   192‚Üí## Validation Results
   193‚Üí- Schema: {pass/fail} (quality score: {score}/100)
   194‚Üí- Frontmatter: {pass/fail}
   195‚Üí- Body: {pass/fail} {split-body applied if applicable}
   196‚Üí- Security: {pass/warn/skipped}
   197‚Üí- Content Quality (tessl): {pass/warn/skipped} (score: {score}%)
   198‚Üí- Metadata: {pass/fail}
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí## Quality Score Breakdown
   201‚Üí- Frontmatter (30%): {score} | Description (30%): {score} | Body (20%): {score} | Links (10%): {score} | File (10%): {score}
   202‚Üí
   203‚Üí## Auto-Fixed Issues
   204‚Üí- {list of issues automatically corrected by --fix}
   205‚Üí
   206‚Üí## Remaining Warnings / Security Findings / Content Quality (tessl)
   207‚Üí- {warnings, security results, tessl scores and suggestions ‚Äî or "skipped"}
   208‚Üí```
   209‚Üí
   210‚Üí### 9. Menu Handling Logic
   211‚Üí
   212‚Üí**Conditional interaction step.** If tessl produced suggestions, section 6b halts for user input. Otherwise, auto-proceed.
   213‚Üí
   214‚ÜíAfter validation completes (including any user decisions from section 6b), immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   215‚Üí
   216‚Üí- Tool unavailability is a skip, not a halt
   217‚Üí- Validation failures are warnings ‚Äî proceed to artifact generation
   218‚Üí- tessl gate only triggers when suggestions exist ‚Äî no gate for clean reviews or unavailable tools
   219‚Üí
   220‚Üí## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   221‚Üí
   222‚ÜíONLY WHEN validation is complete (or skipped) and evidence-report content is updated will you proceed to load `{nextStepFile}` for artifact generation.
   223‚Üí
   224‚Üí---
   225‚Üí
   226‚Üí## üö® SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   227‚Üí
   228‚Üí### ‚úÖ SUCCESS:
   229‚Üí
   230‚Üí- `npx skill-check check --fix --format json` executed (or skipped with warning if unavailable)
   231‚Üí- Quality score captured and recorded; auto-fix applied for deterministic issues
   232‚Üí- Split-body applied if `body.max_lines` failed; security scan executed (or skipped with warning)
   233‚Üí- `npx -y tessl skill review` executed (or skipped); content quality warning raised if score < 70%
   234‚Üí- tessl suggestions presented to user when available; user decision recorded
   235‚Üí- TESSL-applied content marked with `<!-- [TESSL:auto-fix] -->` and cited as `[TESSL:suggestion]`
   236‚Üí- Metadata cross-check performed; evidence report updated with structured results
   237‚Üí- Proceeded to step-07 (auto or after user gate)
   238‚Üí
   239‚Üí### ‚ùå SYSTEM FAILURE:
   240‚Üí
   241‚Üí- Halting on validation failure or skill-check unavailability (should warn and proceed)
   242‚Üí- Adding new content during validation without user approval via the tessl gate
   243‚Üí- Applying semantic tessl suggestions without warning the user about unverified content
   244‚Üí- Not recording quality score; skipping security scan without recording the skip
   245‚Üí- Attempting more than one auto-fix cycle per failure
   246‚Üí
   247‚Üí**Master Rule:** Validation informs, it does not block. Record results, fix what's deterministic, scan for security issues, warn about the rest, and proceed.
   248‚Üí

---
name: 'step-06-validate'
description: 'Validate compiled skill content against agentskills.io spec via skill-check'
nextStepFile: './step-07-generate-artifacts.md'
---

# Step 6: Validate

## STEP GOAL:

To validate the compiled SKILL.md content against the agentskills.io specification using skill-check, auto-fix any validation failures, and confirm spec compliance before artifact generation.

## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):

### Universal Rules:

- üìñ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
- üéØ ALWAYS follow the exact instructions in the step file
- ‚öôÔ∏è TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
- ‚úÖ YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`

### Role Reinforcement:

- ‚úÖ You are a skill compilation engine performing quality assurance
- ‚úÖ Validation ensures spec compliance ‚Äî it does not modify content semantics
- ‚úÖ Tool unavailability means skip validation, not halt workflow

### Step-Specific Rules:

- üéØ Focus ONLY on validating compiled content against spec
- üö´ FORBIDDEN to add new content ‚Äî only fix spec compliance issues
- üíæ Validation and auto-fix modify files in the staging directory (`_bmad-output/{name}/`)
- üí¨ If auto-fix fails, report issues clearly but proceed (warn, don't halt)
- ‚öôÔ∏è If skill-check unavailable: skip validation, add warning to evidence report
- ‚öÝÔ∏è Ignore non-zero exit codes from `skill-check` if the JSON output shows 0 errors ‚Äî parse JSON output, not exit codes

## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:

- üéØ Follow MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
- üíæ Validation results are added to evidence-report content in context
- üìñ Auto-fix pattern: validate ‚Üí fix ‚Üí re-validate (once)
- üö´ Maximum one auto-fix attempt per validation failure

## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:

- Available: All compiled content from step-05 (SKILL.md, metadata.json, etc.)
- Focus: Spec compliance validation and auto-fix
- Limits: Do NOT add new content or modify extraction data
- Dependencies: Compiled content must exist from step-05

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.

### 1. Check Tool Availability

Run: `npx skill-check -h`

- If succeeds: Continue to automated validation (section 2)
- If fails: Perform manual fallback (section 3); add note to evidence-report: "Spec validation performed manually ‚Äî skill-check tool unavailable"

**Important:** Do not assume availability ‚Äî empirical check required.

### 2. Validate & Auto-Fix (skill-check check --fix)

Run the external skill-check tool against the compiled skill staging directory:

```bash
npx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --fix --format json --no-security-scan
```

This performs frontmatter validation, description quality checks, body limit enforcement, local link resolution, file formatting, auto-fix of deterministic issues, and quality scoring (0-100) across five weighted categories.

**Parse the JSON output** for: `qualityScore` (0-100), `diagnostics[]` (remaining issues), `fixed[]` (auto-corrected issues).

**Note:** `skill-check` may return non-zero exit code even when `errorCount` is 0. Always rely on parsed JSON, not the shell exit code.

- **Score ‚â• 70:** Record "Schema: PASS (score: {score}/100)" in evidence-report
- **Score < 70:** Log remaining diagnostics as warnings, record "Schema: WARN ‚Äî score {score}/100, {count} remaining issues", proceed
- **Unfixable errors:** Record specific rule IDs and suggestions, proceed with warnings

### 3. Validate Frontmatter (Fallback)

**If skill-check was available:** Skip ‚Äî already validated in step 2.

**If skill-check NOT available (fallback):** Perform manual frontmatter compliance check:

- [ ] Frontmatter present ‚Äî file starts with `---` and has closing `---`
- [ ] `name` field ‚Äî present, non-empty, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, 1-64 chars
- [ ] `name` matches skill output directory name
- [ ] `description` field ‚Äî present, non-empty, 1-1024 characters
- [ ] No unknown fields ‚Äî only `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools` permitted
- [ ] `version` and `author` are NOT in frontmatter (they belong in metadata.json)

If fails: auto-fix (deterministic), re-validate once, record result. If passes: record "Frontmatter: PASS".

### 4. Split Oversized Body (if needed)

**If step 2 reported `body.max_lines` failure:**

```bash
npx skill-check split-body <staging-skill-dir> --write
```

Then re-validate: `npx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --format json --no-security-scan`

**If skill-check unavailable or no body size issue:** Skip.

### 5. Security Scan

**If skill-check available:**

```bash
npx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --format json
```

(Security scan enabled by default when `--no-security-scan` omitted. The scan uses [Snyk Agent Scan](https://github.com/snyk/agent-scan) to check for prompt injection risks, sensitive data exposure, and unsafe tool permissions.)

Record any security warnings in evidence-report. Security findings are advisory ‚Äî they do not block artifact generation.

**If security scan fails due to missing SNYK_TOKEN:**

Display actionable guidance:

"Security scan requires a Snyk API token. **Important:** The Snyk API requires an Enterprise plan ([authentication docs](https://docs.snyk.io/snyk-api/authentication-for-api)). To enable:
1. Obtain a Snyk Enterprise account with API access
2. Get your API token from Account Settings ‚Üí API Token
3. Set `SNYK_TOKEN=your-token` in your environment or `.env`
4. Re-run [SF] Setup Forge to update tool detection, then re-run [CS] Create Skill

If you don't have an Enterprise account, use `--no-security-scan` flag in skill-check to skip, or `--security-scan-runner pipx|uvx|local` to control the execution method. Security scanning is optional and does not affect tier level or block skill compilation."

Record: "Security scan skipped ‚Äî SNYK_TOKEN not configured"

**If skill-check unavailable:** Skip with note: "Security scan skipped ‚Äî skill-check tool unavailable"

### 6. Content Quality Review (tessl)

**If tessl available**, run: `npx -y tessl skill review <staging-skill-dir>`

Parse output for: `description_score`, `content_score`, `average_score`, `validation_result`, `judge_suggestions[]`.

- **Content score < 70%:** Record warning: "Content quality warning: tessl scored content at {score}%."
- **Unavailable:** Skip with note: "Content quality review skipped ‚Äî tessl tool unavailable"

**Expected scoring for two-tier design:** The Skill Forge two-tier design (Tier 1 Key API Summary + Tier 2 Full API Reference) intentionally includes progressive disclosure. tessl's `conciseness` scorer may flag this as redundancy (typically scoring 2/3), which is expected behavior ‚Äî not a defect. Content scores >= 60% are acceptable when both tiers are correctly applied with proper differentiation (see step-05 Tier 2 assembly rules). Do NOT consolidate Tier 1 and Tier 2 content to improve the score ‚Äî the two-tier structure is a deliberate design choice for standalone usability.

tessl installs automatically via `npx`. A missing tool is not an error ‚Äî graceful skip.

#### 6b. User Decision Gate (conditional)

**If tessl returned no suggestions OR tessl was unavailable:** Skip this gate ‚Äî auto-proceed.

**If tessl returned suggestions**, present them to the user:

"**Content quality review: {score}%**

tessl suggestions:
{numbered list of judge_suggestions}

**Select an option:**
- **[S] Skip** ‚Äî proceed with current content as-is (default)
- **[A] Apply structural fixes** ‚Äî apply only structural suggestions (split sections, consolidate duplicates). No new content generated.
- **[R] Review all** ‚Äî show each suggestion with proposed changes before applying"

#### Gate Rules:

- **Structural suggestions** (split reference section, consolidate duplicates, reorder sections) can be applied without zero-hallucination risk ‚Äî they restructure existing content
- **Semantic suggestions** (add examples, add error handling, add validation checkpoints) introduce content not verified from source code. If the user chooses to apply these:
  - Warn: "This adds content not verified from source code."
  - Mark applied content with `<!-- [TESSL:auto-fix] -->` markers
  - Cite as `[TESSL:suggestion]` in the provenance map with `confidence: "TESSL"` (below T3)
  - Record in evidence report: "TESSL-suggested content applied: {count} items (unverified)"
- **If user selects [S]:** Record "tessl suggestions: skipped by user" in evidence report. Proceed to section 7.
- **If user selects [A]:** Apply structural fixes only, re-run tessl to capture updated score, record results. Proceed to section 7.
- **If user selects [R]:** Show each suggestion with the proposed change. For each, user confirms or skips. Apply confirmed changes, record results. Proceed to section 7.

### 7. Validate metadata.json

Cross-check metadata.json against extraction inventory:
- `stats.exports_documented` / `stats.exports_total` / `stats.coverage` are accurate
- `confidence_t1`, `confidence_t2`, `confidence_t3` match actual counts
- `spec_version` is "1.3"

Auto-fix any discrepancies (these are computed values).

### 8. Update Evidence Report

Add validation results to evidence-report content in context:

```markdown
## Validation Results
- Schema: {pass/fail} (quality score: {score}/100)
- Frontmatter: {pass/fail}
- Body: {pass/fail} {split-body applied if applicable}
- Security: {pass/warn/skipped}
- Content Quality (tessl): {pass/warn/skipped} (score: {score}%)
- Metadata: {pass/fail}

## Quality Score Breakdown
- Frontmatter (30%): {score} | Description (30%): {score} | Body (20%): {score} | Links (10%): {score} | File (10%): {score}

## Auto-Fixed Issues
- {list of issues automatically corrected by --fix}

## Remaining Warnings / Security Findings / Content Quality (tessl)
- {warnings, security results, tessl scores and suggestions ‚Äî or "skipped"}
```

### 9. Menu Handling Logic

**Conditional interaction step.** If tessl produced suggestions, section 6b halts for user input. Otherwise, auto-proceed.

After validation completes (including any user decisions from section 6b), immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`.

- Tool unavailability is a skip, not a halt
- Validation failures are warnings ‚Äî proceed to artifact generation
- tessl gate only triggers when suggestions exist ‚Äî no gate for clean reviews or unavailable tools

## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE

ONLY WHEN validation is complete (or skipped) and evidence-report content is updated will you proceed to load `{nextStepFile}` for artifact generation.

---

## üö® SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS

### ‚úÖ SUCCESS:

- `npx skill-check check --fix --format json` executed (or skipped with warning if unavailable)
- Quality score captured and recorded; auto-fix applied for deterministic issues
- Split-body applied if `body.max_lines` failed; security scan executed (or skipped with warning)
- `npx -y tessl skill review` executed (or skipped); content quality warning raised if score < 70%
- tessl suggestions presented to user when available; user decision recorded
- TESSL-applied content marked with `<!-- [TESSL:auto-fix] -->` and cited as `[TESSL:suggestion]`
- Metadata cross-check performed; evidence report updated with structured results
- Proceeded to step-07 (auto or after user gate)

### ‚ùå SYSTEM FAILURE:

- Halting on validation failure or skill-check unavailability (should warn and proceed)
- Adding new content during validation without user approval via the tessl gate
- Applying semantic tessl suggestions without warning the user about unverified content
- Not recording quality score; skipping security scan without recording the skip
- Attempting more than one auto-fix cycle per failure

**Master Rule:** Validation informs, it does not block. Record results, fix what's deterministic, scan for security issues, warn about the rest, and proceed.

# SKILL.md Section Structure

## agentskills.io Compliant Format

### Frontmatter (Required ‚Äî agentskills.io Compliance)

```yaml
---
name: {skill-name}
description: >
  {Trigger-optimized description of what the skill does and when to use it.
  Include specific keywords for agent discovery.
  Mention what NOT to use it for if applicable.
  1-1024 characters.}
---
```

**Frontmatter rules (agentskills.io specification):**

- `name`: 1-64 characters, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, must match parent directory name
- `description`: 1-1024 characters, trigger-optimized for agent matching
- Only 6 fields permitted: `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools`
- `version` and `author` belong in metadata.json, NOT in frontmatter

### Two-Tier Section Structure

SKILL.md uses a two-tier structure to ensure actionable content survives `split-body` extraction.

#### Tier 1 ‚Äî Always Inline (survives split-body, target <300 lines)

| # | Section | Budget | Purpose |
|---|---------|--------|---------|
| 1 | **Overview** | ~10 lines | What the library does, source repo, version, tier, export count |
| 2 | **Quick Start** | ~30 lines | 3-5 core functions with one runnable end-to-end example |
| 3 | **Common Workflows** | ~30 lines | 4-5 typical function call sequences for common tasks |
| 4 | **Key API Summary** | ~20 lines | Table of top 10-15 functions (name, purpose, key params) |
| 4b | **Migration & Deprecation Warnings** | ~10 lines | T2-future warnings inline (Deep tier only, skip if none) |
| 5 | **Key Types** | ~20 lines | Most important enum/type values inline |
| 6 | **Architecture at a Glance** | ~10 lines | Bullet list of subsystem categories |
| 7 | **CLI** | ~10 lines | Basic CLI commands (skip if no CLI) |
| 8 | **Manual Sections** | ~5 lines | `<!-- [MANUAL] -->` markers for update-skill |

#### Tier 2 ‚Äî Reference-Eligible (extracted by split-body into references/)

| # | Section | Purpose |
|---|---------|---------|
| 9 | **Full API Reference** | Complete signatures, parameter tables, return types, examples, citations |
| 10 | **Full Type Definitions** | All types, interfaces, enums with full field details |
| 11 | **Full Integration Patterns** | Co-import patterns, adapter details, pipeline internals (Forge/Deep only) |

#### Assembly Rules

- Tier 1 sections are assembled first ‚Äî they form the standalone body
- Tier 2 sections are assembled after ‚Äî they are progressive disclosure detail
- Tier 1 sections are kept short enough that `split-body` targets the larger Tier 2 sections (`## Full` headings) into `references/`
- After split-body, SKILL.md retains all Tier 1 content ‚Äî actionable without loading references
- An agent loading only SKILL.md (no references) must get enough to act
- **Section 4b (Migration & Deprecation Warnings)** is conditional: only emitted for Deep tier when T2-future annotations exist. Quick/Forge tiers and Deep tiers without T2-future annotations omit it entirely (no empty section). Parsers and validators must treat this section as optional.

### Provenance Citation Format

| Tier | Format | Example |
|------|--------|---------|
| T1 (AST) | `[AST:{file}:L{line}]` | `[AST:src/auth/index.ts:L42]` |
| T1-low (Source) | `[SRC:{file}:L{line}]` | `[SRC:src/auth/index.ts:L42]` |
| T2 (QMD) | `[QMD:{collection}:{doc}]` | `[QMD:project:CHANGELOG.md]` |
| T3 (External) | `[EXT:{url}]` | `[EXT:docs.example.com/api]` |

### [MANUAL] Section Markers

Seed empty manual sections for future update-skill compatibility:

```markdown
<!-- [MANUAL:additional-notes] -->
<!-- Add custom notes here. This section is preserved during skill updates. -->
<!-- [/MANUAL:additional-notes] -->
```

Place after Quick Start and after API Reference sections.

---

## context-snippet.md Format (Vercel-aligned indexed format)

Indexed pipe-delimited format for CLAUDE.md managed section (~80-120 tokens per skill). Aligned with Vercel's research finding that retrieval instructions + indexed file maps + inline gotchas dramatically improve agent performance.

```markdown
[{skill-name} v{version}]|root: skills/{skill-name}/
|IMPORTANT: {skill-name} v{version} ‚Äî read SKILL.md before writing {skill-name} code. Do NOT rely on training data.
|quick-start:{SKILL.md#quick-start}
|api: {top exports with () for functions, comma-separated}
|key-types:{SKILL.md#key-types} ‚Äî {inline summary of most important type values}
|gotchas: {2-3 most critical pitfalls or breaking changes, inline}
```

### Format rules

- **Line 1:** Skill name + version + root path ‚Äî version signals training data staleness
- **Line 2 (IMPORTANT):** Retrieval instruction ‚Äî always present, tells agent to read SKILL.md before acting
- **Lines 3+:** Pipe-delimited index mapping topics to SKILL.md sections (with `#anchor` pointers)
- **Inline content:** Each index line includes a brief inline summary (~10 words) so the agent can decide whether to read the full section
- **gotchas line:** 2-3 most critical pitfalls inline ‚Äî prevents mistakes without requiring a file read
- **Token budget:** ~80-120 tokens per skill (justified by Vercel's finding that indexed format maintains performance at 80% compression)
- **T1-now content only** ‚Äî no T2 annotations in the snippet
- **Section anchors** (`#quick-start`, `#key-types`) must match actual SKILL.md heading slugs
- **Version** comes from source detection (per issue #32), not brief default

---

## metadata.json Structure

```json
{
  "name": "{skill-name}",
  "version": "{source-version}",
  "skill_type": "single",
  "source_authority": "{official|community|internal}",
  "source_repo": "{github-url}",
  "source_root": "{resolved-source-path}",
  "source_commit": "{commit-hash}",
  "confidence_tier": "{Quick|Forge|Deep}",
  "spec_version": "1.3",
  "generation_date": "{ISO-8601}",
  "exports": [],
  "tool_versions": {
    "ast_grep": "{version-or-null}",
    "qmd": "{version-or-null}",
    "skf": "1.0.0"
  },
  "stats": {
    "exports_documented": 0,
    "exports_total": 0,
    "coverage": 0.0,
    "confidence_t1": 0,
    "confidence_t2": 0,
    "confidence_t3": 0
  },
  "dependencies": [],
  "compatibility": "{semver-range}"
}
```

---

## references/ Directory Structure

One file per major function group or type:

```
references/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ {function-group-a}.md    ‚Äî Detailed reference with full examples
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ {function-group-b}.md    ‚Äî Detailed reference with full examples
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ {type-name}.md           ‚Äî Type definition details
```

Each reference file includes:
- Full function signatures
- Detailed parameter descriptions
- Return value details
- Complete usage examples
- Related functions cross-references
- Temporal annotations (Deep tier: T2-past, T2-future)

---

## provenance-map.json Structure

```json
{
  "skill_name": "{name}",
  "source_repo": "{url}",
  "source_commit": "{hash}",
  "generated_at": "{ISO-8601}",
  "entries": [
    {
      "claim": "getToken accepts userId and optional TokenOptions",
      "source_file": "src/auth/index.ts",
      "source_line": 42,
      "confidence": "T1",
      "extraction_method": "ast_bridge.scan_definitions",
      "ast_node_type": "export_function_declaration"
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## evidence-report.md Structure

```markdown
# Evidence Report: {skill-name}

**Generated:** {date}
**Forge Tier:** {tier}
**Source:** {repo} @ {commit}

## Tool Versions
- ast-grep: {version}
- QMD: {version}
- SKF: 1.0.0

## Extraction Summary
- Files scanned: {count}
- Exports found: {count}
- Confidence: T1={n}, T2={n}, T3={n}

## Validation Results
- Schema: {pass/fail}
- Frontmatter: {pass/fail}

## Warnings
- {any warnings from extraction or validation}
```

input.new_string:
- Set `stats` from extraction aggregate counts:
  - `exports_public_api`: count of exports from public entry points (`__init__.py`, `index.ts`, `lib.rs`, or equivalent)
  - `exports_internal`: count of all other non-underscore-prefixed exports (internal modules, helpers, adapters)
  - `exports_total`: `exports_public_api` + `exports_internal`
  - `public_api_coverage`: `exports_documented / exports_public_api` (1.0 when all public API exports are documented)
  - `total_coverage`: `exports_documented / exports_total` (may be low for large codebases ‚Äî this is expected)

---
name: 'step-05-compile'
description: 'Assemble SKILL.md, context-snippet, metadata, and references from extraction inventory'
nextStepFile: './step-06-validate.md'
skillSectionsData: '../data/skill-sections.md'
assemblyRulesData: '../data/compile-assembly-rules.md'
---

# Step 5: Compile

## STEP GOAL:

To assemble the complete skill content from the extraction inventory and enrichment annotations ‚Äî building SKILL.md sections, context-snippet.md, metadata.json, and references/ content according to the agentskills.io format.

## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):

### Universal Rules:

- üìñ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
- üéØ ALWAYS follow the exact instructions in the step file
- ‚öôÔ∏è TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
- ‚úÖ YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`

### Role Reinforcement:

- ‚úÖ You are a skill compilation engine performing structured assembly
- ‚úÖ Every instruction in SKILL.md must trace to source code with a provenance citation
- ‚úÖ Uncitable content is excluded, not guessed ‚Äî zero hallucination

### Step-Specific Rules:

- üéØ Focus ONLY on assembling content from extraction inventory + enrichment
- üö´ FORBIDDEN to include any content without a provenance citation
- üíæ Sub-agents and compilation processes may write to a staging directory (e.g., `_bmad-output/{name}/`)
- üö´ FORBIDDEN to write final files to `skills/` or `forge-data/` ‚Äî that's step-07
- üö´ FORBIDDEN to fabricate examples not found in source tests or docs
- ‚öíÔ∏è Seed `<!-- [MANUAL] -->` markers for future update-skill compatibility

## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:

- üéØ Follow MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
- üíæ Build all content in context or write to a staging directory (`_bmad-output/{name}/`)
- üìñ Follow agentskills.io section structure from data file
- üö´ Do not promote any output files to final `skills/` or `forge-data/` directories ‚Äî that's step-07

## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:

- Available: extraction_inventory, enrichment_annotations (if Deep), doc_fetch_inventory (if doc_urls), brief_data, tier
- Focus: Assembling structured content from verified data
- Docs-only mode: If `source_type: "docs-only"`, all content is T3 `[EXT:{url}]`. Overview must note "Generated from external documentation." metadata.json `source_authority` forced to `community`. No AST/source citations exist ‚Äî only `[EXT:...]` citations.
- Limits: Do NOT write to final `skills/` or `forge-data/` directories, validate spec compliance, or report
- Dependencies: Extraction inventory from step-03 (enrichment from step-04 if Deep)

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.

### 1. Load Data Files

Load `{skillSectionsData}` and `{assemblyRulesData}` completely. These define the agentskills.io-compliant format and detailed assembly rules for all output artifacts.

### 2. Build SKILL.md Content

Assemble each section in order using the assembly rules data file (`{assemblyRulesData}`). The data file specifies frontmatter format, Tier 1 section details (Sections 1-8), Tier 2 section details (Sections 9-11), and assembly ordering rules. Follow it exactly.

### 3. Build context-snippet.md Content

Vercel-aligned indexed format for CLAUDE.md managed section (~80-120 tokens):

```markdown
[{skill-name} v{version}]|root: skills/{skill-name}/
|IMPORTANT: {skill-name} v{version} ‚Äî read SKILL.md before writing {skill-name} code. Do NOT rely on training data.
|quick-start:{SKILL.md#quick-start}
|api: {top exports with () for functions, comma-separated}
|key-types:{SKILL.md#key-types} ‚Äî {inline summary of most important type values}
|gotchas: {2-3 most critical pitfalls or breaking changes, inline}
```

**Derivation rules:**

- **version**: From source detection (reconciled in step-03), not brief default
- **api**: Top 10 exports from extraction inventory, append `()` to function names
- **key-types**: Inline summary of most important enum/type values from Key Types section
- **gotchas**: Derived from T2-future annotations (breaking changes), async requirements, version-specific behavior changes. If no gotchas available, omit the gotchas line.
- **Section anchors** (`#quick-start`, `#key-types`): Must match actual heading slugs in the assembled SKILL.md

### 4. Build metadata.json Content

Following the structure from the skill-sections data file:
- Populate all fields from brief_data, extraction inventory, and tier
- Set `generation_date` to current ISO-8601 timestamp
- Set `source_commit` from resolved source (if available)
- Set `stats` from extraction aggregate counts
- Set `tool_versions` based on tier and available tools

### 5. Build references/ Content

Create one reference file per major function group or type:
- Full function signatures with detailed parameter descriptions
- Complete usage examples (from source only)
- Related functions cross-references
- Temporal annotations (Deep tier: T2-past, T2-future)

Group functions logically by module, file, or functional area.

### 6. Build provenance-map.json Content

One entry per extracted claim: claim text, source_file, source_line, confidence tier (T1/T1-low/T2), extraction_method, ast_node_type. See `{skillSectionsData}` for full schema.

### 7. Build evidence-report.md Content

Compilation audit trail: generation date, forge tier, source info, tool versions, extraction summary (files/exports/confidence), validation results (populated in step-06), warnings. See `{skillSectionsData}` for full template.

### 8. Menu Handling Logic

**Auto-proceed step ‚Äî no user interaction.**

After all content is assembled in context (or written to the staging directory), immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`.

#### EXECUTION RULES:

- This is an auto-proceed assembly step with no user choices
- All content stays in context or in the staging directory ‚Äî no final files are written yet
- Proceed directly to validation after assembly is complete

## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE

ONLY WHEN all 6 content artifacts (SKILL.md, context-snippet.md, metadata.json, references/, provenance-map.json, evidence-report.md) are assembled in context will you proceed to load `{nextStepFile}` for spec validation. Note: extraction-rules.yaml is generated by step-07 from extraction data, not assembled here.

---

## üö® SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS

### ‚úÖ SUCCESS:

- Skill-sections data file and assembly rules data file loaded and followed
- SKILL.md assembled with all required sections in agentskills.io format
- Tier 1 sections assembled first and kept under 300 lines (excluding frontmatter)
- Tier 2 sections assembled after all Tier 1 sections
- Section 4b emitted only when Deep tier AND T2-future annotations exist; omitted entirely otherwise (no empty section)
- Every function entry has a provenance citation
- [MANUAL] markers seeded for update-skill compatibility
- context-snippet.md, metadata.json, references/, provenance-map.json, evidence-report.md all assembled
- No content without provenance citations included
- Auto-proceeded to step-06

### ‚ùå SYSTEM FAILURE:

- Including functions or examples without provenance citations
- Fabricating usage examples not found in source
- Writing files to final `skills/` or `forge-data/` directories (that's step-07)
- Missing required SKILL.md sections
- Emitting an empty Section 4b when no T2-future annotations exist
- Tier 1 content exceeding 300 lines without reduction
- Assembling Tier 2 sections before Tier 1 sections
- Not seeding [MANUAL] markers
- Not building all 6 content artifacts (extraction-rules.yaml is built by step-07)

**Master Rule:** Zero hallucination ‚Äî every line of SKILL.md must trace to source code. Compile from data, not imagination.

lines: - Populate all fields from brief_data, extraction inventory, and tier

+  - `public_api_coverage`: `exports_documented / exports_public_api` (1.0 when all public API exports are documented)
+  - `total_coverage`: `exports_documented / exports_total` (may be low for large codebases ‚Äî this is expected)
